members.name: Dr. Apeksha Ekbote Dr. Apeksha
members.details: Ekbote is Chief Dietitian at NephroPlus, leading nutritional care for dialysis patients across India for the past five years. She is a Ph.D. in Nutrition from Osmania University, Registered Dietitian, and a certified Diabetes Educator. A double gold medalist in her master’s program in Nutrition, Dr. Apeksha Ekbote’s professional interests lie in Renal Nutrition, Critical Care, Metabolic Disorders, and Weight Management. A true polyglot, she is fluent in eight languages. Beyond her professional pursuits, she enjoys art and craft, baking, and continuously enhancing her knowledge—most recently exploring the intersection of nutrition and artificial intelligence.

members.details: Nancy Sahni along with the organizing Committee of the Department of Dietetics, PGIMER Chandigarh, celebrated World Kidney Day, 2026 The participants showcased their culinary skills in doling out kidney friendly dishes . The initiative highlighted the often under- recognized role of combining art and science in designing renal failure friendly therapeutic dishes for kidney disease patients and making them attractive , palatable as well as scientifically balanced according to high energy, low protein and balanced electrolytes demand of the disease. The competition broke the myth of such recipes to be bland and uninteresting that may further push the renal failure patient towards malnutrition and poorer quality of life. Recipes consisted of renal friendly snacks, beverages and main meals. d. Mention if the event was conducted in collaboration with any organization (Optional) :- It was done under the banner of IAPEN , CHANDIGARH CHAPTER. a. Name & Place of the Activity :-Dietetics Department, GMSH-16 Chandigarh celebrated World Kidney Day, 2026 on the theme “Kidney Health for All: Caring for people , protecting the planet”. b. Date of the Event :- 12 th March, 2026 c. Brief Description Members/Participants Involved :- Dietician Mandeep developed meal plans for patients undergoing dialysis. Dietician Komal provided nutritional guidelines aimed at reducing the risk of kidney-related complications among diabetic, hypertensive and renal calculi patients. Diet charts were distributed to approximately 50–70 beneficiaries. d. Mention if the event was conducted in collaboration with any organization (Optional) :- It was done under the banner of IAPEN , CHANDIGARH CHAPTER. a. Name & Place of the Activity :-Nutrition & Dietetics Department, Chandigarh Group of College (CGC), Landran celebrated World Kidney Day, 2026 on the theme “Kidney Health for All: Caring for people , protecting the planet”. b. Date of the Event :- 12 th March, 2026 c. Brief Description Members/Participants Involved :- Students of B.Sc. Nutrition and Dietetics prepared kidney-friendly recipes to promote awareness about renal health. A total of 22 entries were received, and after evaluation, first, second, and third prizes were awarded to the best recipes. The activity encouraged students to apply their theoretical knowledge in a practical and creative manner while highlighting the importance of balanced nutrition for kidney care. It also helped raise awareness among participants and attendees about adopting healthy dietary habits to prevent renal complications. d. He is known for his patient-centric approach, collaborative work ethic, and commitment to academic excellence in the field of critical care.
